answer_text: Since 2020/21 the Department has introduced part-time 20mph speed limits at 215 schools.	The work was completed in two tranches and represents an investment of about £4m.	A review is underway to determine if a further tranche is justified.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Agriculture, Environment and Rural Affairs whether his Department has made representations to the Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs in relation to securing full compensation for farmers who are unable to export livestock from Great Britain due to the bluetongue outbreak.
answer_text: My officials regularly engage with their counterparts across DEFRA and the Devolved Administrations on the response to, and impacts of, the incursion of Bluetongue in Great Britain. They have confirmed that a compensation scheme, in respect of animals that cannot now be certified for moves from Great Britian to Northern Ireland, is not currently being considered.

answer_text: I have placed the Brookeborough Shared Education Campus (SEC) project into my Department’s conventional major works programme to continue to advance in planning.  Work is ongoing to finalise the business case for the project.

question_text: To ask the First Minister and deputy First Minister for an update on the delivery of the Together: Building a United Community strategy.
answer_text: There has been significant progress in delivering the Together: Building a United Community Strategy since its implementation.Over 30,000 young people have taken part in 870 T:BUC Camps. Five Urban Villages areas have been established and the target of building 10 shared neighbourhoods has been met. Over 27,000 participants took part in the Uniting Communities programme of sporting events and young leader training. Over 6,000 young people participated in the United Youth programme. The first Shared Education Campus at Limavady is now operational, with work expected to commence soon on the Ballycastle Shared Education Campus.Approximately one third of interface barriers have been removed, with a further third reduced in stature. Work continues across other interface sites to secure the conditions needed for reduction or removal.Officials are currently conducting a review of the T:BUC Strategy and we recognise the ongoing importance of Good Relations in creating a place where everyone can prosper.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the Ground Works Scheme, Minor Works Programme, in relation to St. Ninnidh's Primary School, Derrylin.
answer_text: On 8 February 2024, St Ninnidh’s Primary School agreed to the separation of the grounds works scheme into two projects, of which the repurposing of the entrance area at the front of the school as a second hard play area would be prioritised.  Subject to business case approval and capital budget availability, it is anticipated that work on this project will commence early in 2024/25.  The drainage works scheme to the grass play area at the rear of the school is currently not being prioritised for delivery due to the many competing priorities on the minor works programme, the significant cost associated with the scheme and my Department's restricted capital budget position.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the School Enhancement Programme in relation to St. Kevin's College, Lisnaskea.
answer_text: The design team appointed to the St Kevin’s College project is currently developing RIBA Stage 4 (Technical Design), with this stage due to complete in April 2024.  This is the final design stage before construction tender. Approval to proceed to construction will be dependent on the availability of capital funding. I will continue to bid for additional capital funding and make the case for increased investment in the education estate.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the new Lisnaskea Health and Care Centre.
answer_text: Design work on the Lisnaskea Health and Care Centre project has been completed and the construction works have been tendered.  The tender closed on 19th March 2024. The returned tenders are currently being evaluated.Construction work is scheduled to commence on site in Summer 2024, subject to successful procurement outcomes and Full Business Case approval.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education whether she plans a further increase in the daily rates of home to school transport or meal deliveries to contracted operators, given the recent rise in fuel costs.
answer_text: In April this year, the Education Authority (EA) awarded a 5% increase to Private Operators delivering home to school transport and school meals for the remainder of the 2021/22 school year in acknowledgement of the pressures many contractors were experiencing due to the rising costs of fuel. The 5% increase was based on an analysis of published prices of fuel at the pump.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Education for an update on the new Carrosyl Community Centre in Lisnaskea.
answer_text: The Departmental Solicitor’s Office is in the process of completing the legal due diligence for the Carrosyl Community Centre scheme.  A small number of issues remain outstanding which are currently with Carrosyl’s solicitors to be addressed.  It is envisaged that the scheme will be on site in this financial year, subject to the completion of the legal due diligence and tender process to my Department’s satisfaction.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health what action he has taken to ensure families are not left out of pocket due to the shortfall in direct payments for domiciliary care.
answer_text: Direct payment funding is provided in lieu of statutory provided services and is calculated on the basis of individually assessed need. The model allows individuals to decide how their needs are met, by whom, and when. This could mean taking on the role of employer, and employing their own Personal Assistant using a dedicated direct payment budget provided by their Trust. In November last year I announced a £23m support package which was primarily designed as a response to the pressures facing the domiciliary care and care home workforces. This investment allowed providers to deliver enhanced rates of pay and improve other terms and conditions, There has been positive feedback on how this additional funding has helped to stabilise the care sector during a very challenging period. Despite a very challenging financial context I have ensured that funding was secured to ensure this uplift was carried through to the current year and has been made recurrent.While this funding package did not extend to direct payments, I am actively considering if there would be scope to address direct payment rates which are an extremely important part of our care system, particularly in allowing for a person centred approach. As you are aware however, we face and are likely to continue to face, an extremely challenging fiscal environment and the absence of an agreed three year budget makes this position even more challenging.My Department has recently undertaken a public consultation on proposals to Reform the Adult Social Care system in Northern Ireland. The consultation document included a number of proposals in relation to self-directed support and direct payments. Now that the consultation period has ended, my officials are analysing the responses and a summary and evaluation report will be prepared for Ministerial consideration. The Department will publish the consultation report, which will then inform the development of a new strategy for adult social care. I look forward to seeing the outcome of the consultation and views in relation to the proposals on self-directed support and direct payments.

question_text: To ask the Minister of Health for an update on the Lisnaskea Health Centre project.
answer_text: The Western Health and Social Care Trust have advised that the design for the new Lisnaskea Health and Care Centre commenced in January 2022 and this phase is scheduled to complete in November 2022.The new health and care centre will provide a central hub in Lisnaskea and will enable linkages with rural practices. This will support a network of 16 GPs and will provide services to a combined practice list of approximately 25,000 people. The new 3,500m2 facility will bring services together under one roof including GP, Trust Community and Day Care services. This will provide improved accessibility and increased provision of services in Lisnaskea such as Podiatry, Speech and Language Therapy, Physiotherapy, Mental Health services and Older People services.
